action: Do not throw in normal dustbin. Store safely and give to an e-waste collection point.
tip: Batteries can leak harmful chemicals if mixed with household waste.
journey: Collected safely;Sent to authorized e-waste center;Metals are recovered;Toxic leakage is prevented
warning: High safety alert: keep away from fire and water.

action: Dispose in wet waste or composting unit if safe.
tip: Organic waste can become compost and reduce landfill smell.
journey: Collected in wet bin;Sent to composting unit;Converted into compost;Used for soil improvement
warning: Do not mix with dry recyclable waste.

action: Handle carefully and place in recyclable glass bin.
tip: Glass can be recycled many times without major quality loss.
journey: Sorted by color;Crushed into cullet;Melted in furnace;Made into new glass products
warning: Wrap broken glass before disposal to avoid injury.

action: Flatten and keep dry before recycling.
tip: Dry cardboard has higher recycling value.
journey: Collected dry;Sorted and baled;Pulped;Converted into packaging material
warning: Do not mix wet or food-stained cardboard.

action: Donate if usable or send to textile recycling.
tip: Old clothes can be reused, upcycled, or converted into fibers.
journey: Collected separately;Sorted by condition;Donated or shredded;Reused as textile fiber
warning: Do not throw usable clothes into general trash.

action: Place carefully in glass recycling bin.
journey: Sorted by color;Crushed into cullet;Melted;Made into bottles or jars

action: Rinse containers and place in dry recycling bin.
tip: Metal recycling saves energy compared to producing new metal.
journey: Collected dry;Magnetically sorted;Melted;Converted into new metal products
warning: Sharp metal edges should be covered.

action: Keep paper clean and dry before recycling.
tip: Paper recycling reduces tree usage and landfill volume.
journey: Collected dry;Sorted by grade;Pulped;Made into recycled paper
warning: Food-stained paper should go to wet/general waste.

tip: Clean plastic is easier to recycle and reuse.
journey: Collected from dry bin;Sorted by plastic type;Shredded into flakes;Reused for products or fibers
warning: Avoid burning plastic because it releases harmful fumes.

action: Donate usable shoes or send damaged pairs to recycling where available.
tip: Shoes can be reused by others if still wearable.
journey: Collected separately;Checked for reuse;Donated or processed;Materials recovered
warning: Do not mix reusable items with wet waste.

action: Dispose as residual waste if it cannot be reused or recycled.
tip: Reduce trash by choosing reusable products when possible.
journey: Collected as residual waste;Sent for processing;Non-recyclables handled safely;Landfill load monitored
warning: Try to avoid mixing recyclable items with trash.

action: Place in glass recycling bin and handle safely.
tip: Clear glass is highly recyclable when kept separate.
journey: Sorted by color;Crushed into cullet;Melted;Made into new clear glass products
warning: Wrap sharp broken pieces before disposal.

action: Dispose in wet waste bin or composting unit.
tip: Food waste can become compost instead of producing landfill methane.
journey: Collected in green bin;Composted;Converted into nutrient-rich compost;Used for plants

action: Give to authorized e-waste collection centers.
tip: Electronics contain valuable metals and harmful substances.
journey: Collected separately;Sent to certified recycler;Components separated;Metals recovered safely
warning: Do not dismantle electronics without proper safety.
